home.localTimerDescription: Use the timer directly in your browser.
home.liveSessionDescription: Keep multiple devices synchronized in real time.
home.trustLine: No account required · Local-first · Open source
home.twoWaysSubtitle: One setup, two ways to run it — keep the timer to yourself, or bring others along in real time.
home.localTimerDescription: Run the timer you just set up. Nothing to share, nothing to sign up for.
home.localFeature1: No account — just open it and start
home.localFeature4: Ideal for focus, workouts and study
home.liveSessionDescription: Take the same setup live and keep every screen in sync — no installs for anyone.
home.liveStep1: Create a session in one tap
home.liveStep2: Share the join link with your group
home.liveStep3: Keep the private manage link to run it
home.liveStep4: Edits sync to every screen instantly
home.twoWaysClosing: Built for workshops, facilitation and classrooms — and just as good for sports, training, study sessions, meetings and everyday focus.
home.privateByDesignBody: Usable without an account. Local timers stay on your device; live state is shared only with people who have the join or manage link.
home.openSourceBody: The code is public on GitHub under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Contributions, feedback, accessibility suggestions and bug reports are welcome.
home.fromMkrzLabBody: A side project by Tobias Zucali. mkrz lab designs and facilitates workshops and collaborative learning where making becomes a tool for participation.
